Mainnet Control Plane and Sidecar Hardening

Generated: 2026-06-07 00:55 SAST

This report captures the active hardening pass after the public-chain divergence and restart-control incidents. The operating target is one mainnet mining node, one mining pool, deterministic control surfaces, and low-priority sidecars that preserve recovery options without competing with mining.

What Changed

AreaCurrent BehaviorEvidence
Mainnet-only stack paths Rawdatadir, IPFS segment writer, sidecar sealing, restore, and installer-generated service env now reject non-mainnet network values and normalize to mainnet. Active source and live runtime searches for forbidden non-mainnet network labels returned no matches outside ignored runtime/data/dashboard/generated paths.
Public-chain safety proof Status collection now records a canonical_mining_safety payload from local/public EVM samples. Hash mismatch is retained as diagnostics for this node build; safety requires public reference evidence, miner/sample alignment, no public divergence, and no solo-mining signature. Live dashboard status after sampler restart: overall=ok, mode=mining, can_mine=true, four miners, no sync warnings, canonical_safe=true.
Shared pool start gate Automated pool starts require the shared gate and a positive canonical safety proof. Missing proof now fails closed. Focused unit tests cover synced/no-proof blocking and ready/proof-available allow paths.
Running-pool containment Status sampler can stop a running pool on explicit public-chain divergence or solo-mining suspicion and records the containment reason. Focused tests cover pool running + public divergence => containment stop.
Automation control Status-sampler config edits, node recreates, and systemd starts are now checked through automation-control action classes before mutating runtime config, containers, or user services. Focused automation-control tests prove config edit, recreate, and systemd start are suppressed when control permission is absent.
Installer first start Linux installers now provision automation control and start postgres node dashboard first. They no longer include pool in the initial compose startup command. Deployment portability tests and the restart-hardening validator require sync-only first start.
Idle pool autostart Status sampler no longer starts an idle synced pool by default. A stopped pool requires miner demand or a visible ASIC LAN candidate plus a passing shared gate. Status-sampler tests cover idle synced pool staying stopped and miner-demand starts using the compose/gate path.
Node mining/template repair Status sampler now requires the shared canonical gate before enabling node mining/template env, recreates nodes with --no-build --pull never, and does not enable modules=miner or miner=true in node.conf. Regression tests block missing-canonical config edits and assert node.conf miner/module lines are not enabled.
IPFS content sidecar network guard The IPFS content sidecar now blocks finalized artifact manifests that do not declare mainnet before publishing an index. Regression test covers signed non-mainnet manifest rejection using neutral fixture naming.
Low-priority rawdatadir sidecar The sidecar remains lazy/retrying, mainnet-only, nice/ionice lowered, systemd resource-weighted, and rsync bandwidth-limited to 4096 KiB/s by default. It no longer uses rsync hard-link tracking and keeps open hard-linked restore points before refreshing the mutable mirror. Live service command uses rsync -a ... --bwlimit 4096 without -H or default --delay-updates. Process RSS is small; cgroup memory is mostly file cache accounting during I/O.
Status truthfulness Selected backend source-health degradation is advisory when recent accepted block submissions prove the pool is still submitting blocks. This avoids showing a false sync/down warning while mining is demonstrably active. Focused tests cover advisory degradation with recent paid work. Live status settled at ok/mining.

Important Boundaries

  • The active pool stack is mainnet-only. Wider workspace scans still find non-mainnet references in core protocol repositories, old research worktrees, backups, and vendored geth data. Those were not blindly rewritten because that would be a separate protocol-level change with consensus and test implications.
  • The current stack-level proof is a containment and orchestration guard. The deeper architecture still needs a corechain and pool job-level canonical safety lease so template/job/submit paths cannot exist without fresh public-mainnet safety evidence.
  • The sidecar is a recovery aid and artifact source, not a consensus proof. Restore or publish promotion must remain gated by independent chain validation.

Remaining Architecture Work

  1. Corechain mining safety lease: add a public-mainnet safety snapshot with external witness evidence, same-height DAG/EVM anchors, expiry, generation, quarantine reason, and chain ID. Gate every template and submit ingress before mining work is produced or accepted.
  2. Pool job safety lease: attach the canonical safety lease to fetched templates and Stratum jobs. Cached templates, rebroadcast, recovery, submit, and fanout must hard-veto missing, stale, wrong-network, or unsafe leases.
  3. Installer sequencing: install and start node/dashboard sync-only first, rebuild plot/global/wallet data with a visible dashboard holding screen, then start the pool only through the shared pool gate after canonical safety passes.
  4. Single mutation broker: continue reducing direct Docker/systemd/config mutations so watchdog, sampler, sentinel, peer updater, snapshot, and sidecar scripts submit one audited mutation intent rather than independently fighting for control.
  5. Append-only recovery ledger: separate the mutable rsync mirror from an append-only local ledger of safe points and signed/IPFS-ready immutable segment generations.
  6. Workspace cleanup policy: decide whether non-mainnet protocol references in corechain and research worktrees are product scope. If yes, do it as an explicit consensus/protocol cleanup project with tests, not as an incidental pool-stack patch.

GitHub Tracking

  • #51: public-mainnet canonical mining safety lease across corechain and pool jobs.
  • #52: installers sync-only first, then pool only through the shared gate.
  • #53: append-only recovery ledger for rawdatadir/IPFS sidecar safe points.
  • #54: centralize stack mutations behind one audited broker.

Validation Run

Focused Python validation passed with temporary runtime directories and bytecode disabled: 158 tests covered rawdatadir sidecar safety, IPFS segment writer, IPFS content sidecar, chain-state self-heal mainnet-only behavior, chain RPC resilience, pool start gate, status sampler mining imperative, deployment portability, rawdatadir sidecar finalization/verify, automation control, pool efficiency loss ledger, and no-miner status collection.

Static release validation also passed: release bootstrap static tests, rawdatadir artifact static tests, doc consistency check, and restart-hardening validator.

Live validation after restarting the sampler showed overall ok, mode mining, can_mine true, four connected miners, fresh accepted block submit/share data, no sync warnings, and canonical safety true.
